content.subtitle: Use
rows: To express wishes about now or generally
Carl wishes he had a telescope.
I wish I wasn't scared of spiders.
To express wishes about the past
Tracy wishes she'd seen that programme about the moon last night.
I wish they hadn't cut down so many trees.
To criticise other people, or to complain about something
I wish people would throw their litter in the bin.
I wish you wouldn't smoke in here.
To express wishes about ability and permission now or in the future
I wish I could travel through time!
David wishes he could come with us.

content.subtitle: Extra notes
items: With wish + past simple, we can say I/he/she/it was or were. Were is more formal: I wish I was an astronaut. / I wish I were an astronaut.
We can use if only in the same way as wish: If only I was/were an astronaut.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: We don't use would for wishes about ourselves: I wish I lived on Mars. (not I wish I would live on Mars.)
Use wish for unreal situations. If something may happen in the future, use hope: I hope it doesn't snow tomorrow.

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct form
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence using wish and the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... (have) more free time.
Sam wishes he ...... (not be) so shy.
We wish we ...... (can) speak Japanese.
She wishes she ...... (study) harder for the exam.
I wish my neighbours ...... (not play) loud music at night.
Tom wishes he ...... (not forget) his umbrella yesterday.
I wish I ...... (be) taller.
Mia wishes she ...... (can come) with us next weekend.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Choose the best option for each sentence.

items.sentence: I wish I **know / knew / had known** the answer now.
They wish they **wouldn't be / weren't / hadn't been** late every day.
They wish they **can / could / had been able** drive.
I wish you **stop / would stop / had stopped** shouting.
She wishes she **saw / had seen / would see** the film last night.
If only they **are / were / had been** better at maths.

sections.title: C: Rewrite with wish or if only
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence so it keeps the same meaning.

items.sentence: I don't have a bike. → I ......
items.answer: I wish I had a bike.

items.sentence: My brother never tidies his room. → I ......
items.answer: I wish my brother would tidy his room.

items.sentence: I didn't bring my dictionary yesterday. → I ......
items.answer: I wish I had brought my dictionary yesterday.

items.sentence: I'm not allowed to stay out late. → I ......
items.answer: I wish I could stay out late.

items.sentence: It may snow tomorrow (possible). → I ......
items.answer: I hope it doesn't snow tomorrow.

items.sentence: I'm not an astronaut. → ......
items.answer: If only I was/were an astronaut.

sections.title: D: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: I wish I would have more money.
items.answer: I wish I had more money.

items.sentence: She wishes she would be taller.
I wish you will stop interrupting me.
items.answer: I wish you would stop interrupting me.

items.sentence: They wish they saw the match last night.
items.answer: They wish they had seen the match last night.

items.sentence: I wish I could passed the test yesterday.
items.answer: I wish I had passed the test yesterday.

items.sentence: I wish it doesn't rain tomorrow.
items.answer: I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow.
